reasoning: Magnus Carlsen's classical rating as of July/August 2025 is ~2839 depending on recent events. To drop below 2800 before 2026 (a ~4-month window), he would have to lose roughly 40 ELO. At a K-factor of 10, this corresponds to underperforming his expected score by 4 full points. Over the course of the few classical games he plays in a 4-month span, this is overwhelmingly unlikely unless he suffers an unprecedented string of losses against much lower-rated opponents. FIDE rules do not penalize inactivity by deducting rating points. Therefore, given the short time horizon and the large 39-point cushion, it is almost impossible for him to drop below 2800 before 2026. Setting probability to 3% to account for unpredictable disastrous scenarios.
